description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a small, isolated test-environment for hostname look-ups.  
A seed “hosts-like” file has already been placed for you at:

    /home/user/qa_env/test_hosts

Your goal is to generate a compact report that associates each hostname found in that file with its corresponding IPv4 address.

Detailed requirements:

1. Create a new directory (if it does not yet exist) called  
   /home/user/qa_dns

2. Inside that directory create the file  

   /home/user/qa_dns/resolved_hosts.log

3. Parse every non-empty, non-comment line of /home/user/qa_env/test_hosts.  
   • Lines are formatted exactly like lines in /etc/hosts:  
     “<IPv4-address><one or more spaces><primary-hostname>[<more aliases>]”  
   • Ignore any alias names that appear after the primary hostname; the report only needs the primary hostname.  
   • Ignore any line that starts with the “#” character (comment).  
   • Ignore blank lines.

4. Produce one output line in resolved_hosts.log for each primary hostname, using **this exact format** (no extra spaces, tabs or delimiters):

      <hostname>:<IPv4-address>

   Example of correct formatting (DON’T copy these literal values—they are only illustrative):

      app.dev.local:10.0.0.11

5. Sort the lines alphabetically by the hostname portion (i.e. lexicographical order, A→Z) before writing them to the log file.

6. The file must end with a single trailing newline character (no blank lines before or after the data).

7. When you are finished, verifying the file with the following command should show identical content (the automated grader will run an equivalent check):

      cat /home/user/qa_dns/resolved_hosts.log

Only 3-4 shell commands should be sufficient to accomplish this task (creating a directory, parsing/sorting, and redirecting output).  
Do not add any additional commentary or text to resolved_hosts.log—only the hostname:IP mappings as specified above.
truth:
Initial state:

1. Directory /home/user/qa_env already exists with permissions 755.  
2. It contains the file /home/user/qa_env/test_hosts whose exact contents (including blank lines) are:

10.10.0.5    api.dev.local api
10.10.0.6    db.dev.local db
10.10.0.7    cache.dev.local cache

# An extra test entry that should be ignored by the agent
# 10.10.0.8    logging.dev.local log

3. Directory /home/user/qa_dns does NOT exist at start.

Expected final state after correct task execution:

1. Directory /home/user/qa_dns has been created (permissions 755 or default umask).  
2. File /home/user/qa_dns/resolved_hosts.log exists, is world-readable (644 or default), and its exact byte-for-byte contents are:

api.dev.local:10.10.0.5
cache.dev.local:10.10.0.7
db.dev.local:10.10.0.6

<newline>

(that is, three lines sorted alphabetically by the hostname, followed by a single trailing newline).
